MOD_DESCRIPTION: Infrastructure survival mod. Manage power grids, schedule outages, protect critical systems.
PRESET_MANAGEDDEFICIT_DESC: Easy mode. 100 MW legal import. Shadow Import cheap ($300/MW). Learn the mechanics.
PRESET_BLACKOUTPROTOCOL_DESC: Recommended. 30 MW legal import. Shadow Import $600/MW. Realistic survival.
PRESET_ISLANDMODE_DESC: Hardcore. 0 MW legal import. Shadow Import $1200/MW. Complete isolation.

PRESET_TOTALCOLLAPSE_DESC: Hardcore. Frostpunk-style survival. Every decision matters.

TOOLTIP_IMPORT_CAP: Limit power imports from outside connections
TOOLTIP_IMPORT_UNLIMITED: Unlimited imports - city buys infinite power from neighbors. NOT RECOMMENDED: mod loses meaning!
TOOLTIP_IMPORT_100: Max 100 MW import - limited external help
TOOLTIP_IMPORT_10: Max 10 MW import - almost isolated grid
TOOLTIP_IMPORT_0: No imports - fully isolated grid, self-sufficient only. RECOMMENDED for challenge!
TOOLTIP_SAFETY_OFF: OFF - no random failures (safety inspectors bribed)
TOOLTIP_BATTERY: Enable battery backup for critical buildings
TOOLTIP_BATTERY_ON: ACTIVE - batteries charge during surplus, discharge during deficit
TOOLTIP_BATTERY_OFF: OFF - no backup power, buildings go dark immediately
TOOLTIP_WINTER_HARSH: HARSH - 3x power consumption in winter months
TOOLTIP_WINTER_MILD: MILD - no seasonal changes to consumption
TOOLTIP_WATER: Link water supply to power grid
TOOLTIP_WATER_LINKED: LINKED - water pumps need power, blackout = no water
TOOLTIP_WATER_FREE: FREE - water works independently of power
WARNING_UNLIMITED_IMPORTS: Unlimited imports ON -- blackouts impossible. Set to 0 for full experience.

MODAL_FIRST_STRIKE_TEXT_1: Your city has been hit. This is not a drill.

MODAL_VICTORY_ENDLESS_DESC: No more victory checks. Play until the end.

TIP_FREQUENCY: Grid frequency. 50 Hz = stable. Below 49 Hz grid starts degrading. Below 48.5 Hz -- collapse imminent
TIP_POWER_BALANCE: Unused generation potential: what your plants can deliver right now minus active load - the same number as the SURPLUS row. Negative = the city's own plants cannot cover the load.
TIP_BATTERY: Backup battery charge level. Discharges during blackouts to power critical buildings
TIP_THRESHOLD: Buildings disconnected by automatic load shedding to prevent grid collapse
TIP_THREAT_PHASE: Current threat phase: Calm -> Alert -> Attack -> Recovery
TIP_CRISIS_ECONOMY: Shock Act restrictions: reduced tax income and frozen loans during crisis
TIP_PRODUCTION: Total power generated by all active power plants
TIP_DEMAND: Total power needed by all connected buildings
TIP_ACTIVE_LOAD: Power actually delivered to buildings after threshold cuts and district sheds
TIP_SHEDDING: Buildings receiving less than 90% of demand are auto-disconnected. Partial power is wasted on critical services (a hospital at 80% does not heal) — the system disconnects under-fed buildings to concentrate capacity where it works. Add production to reduce this.
TIP_MANUAL_OFF: Power not reaching buildings because you disabled districts, categories, or auto-dispatch shed them. Toggle districts back ON in the panel below to restore.
TIP_EXPORT: Legal electricity sales to the outside grid. Each interconnector is capped by the export limit; the outside grid pays for the actual flow. This is NOT the shadow export channel.
TIP_SURPLUS: Unused generation potential: what your plants can deliver right now minus active load. Counts damage, wear, fuel and grid limits - unlike the built MAX, which includes capacity that is unavailable at the moment. Turns yellow when the reserve is smaller than your largest plant: losing it would black out the city.
TIP_BACKUP_BATTERY: Stored energy for emergencies. Recharges when grid has surplus
TIP_GENERATORS: Diesel generators providing backup power. Noisy but reliable
TIP_NOISE: Generator noise level in decibels. High noise reduces citizen happiness
TIP_PROTECTED: Buildings currently receiving backup power during outages
TIP_HOSPITALS: Hospitals with backup power. Unpowered hospitals increase casualties
TIP_SCHOOLS: Schools with backup power. Closed schools reduce citizen wellbeing
TIP_PLANTS_AT_RISK: Power plants with 50%+ wear that are not being repaired. May fail during attacks
TIP_CONSTRUCTION_DELAY: New plants need time to reach full output: Wind 30d, Gas 180d, Nuclear 720d. No power until online
TIP_POLICY_RESERVE: Cold Start -- batteries only activate for full grid collapse. Maximum reserve
TIP_POLICY_CRITICAL: Critical Only -- power hospitals and schools during blackouts
TIP_POLICY_FULL: Full Discharge -- power all buildings with backup connections
TIP_AA_WAVE: Current attack wave number. Each wave gets progressively harder
TIP_AA_THREATS: Total number of incoming threats (drones, missiles) this wave
TIP_AA_INTERCEPTED: Threats successfully shot down by air defense systems
TIP_AA_IMPACTS: Threats that reached their target and caused damage
TIP_AA_INTERCEPT_RATE: Percentage of threats intercepted. Above 80% is excellent
TIP_AA_STATIONS: Number of operational air defense installations
TIP_AA_AMMO: Available interceptor missiles. Depleted = no defense
TIP_TENSION: Enemy activity level. Higher tension means attack is closer
TIP_WAVE_TYPE: Predicted attack type based on intelligence analysis
TIP_ETA: Estimated time until next attack. Insider info gives exact timing
TIP_COMPOSITION: Expected threat mix. Insider info reveals exact numbers
TIP_FOCUS_GENERATION: Enemy priority on power plants. Higher = more missiles targeting generators
TIP_FOCUS_INFRASTRUCTURE: Enemy priority on substations and grid infrastructure
TIP_FOCUS_RESIDENTIAL: Enemy priority on residential areas. Terror strikes cause casualties
TIP_COUNTER_OSINT: Disrupts enemy intelligence gathering. Reduces spotter accuracy and attack precision
TIP_DEFENSE_POLICY: Humanitarian = protect civilian areas first. Grid Defense = protect power infrastructure first
TIP_TRUST: Reputation with shadow market contacts. Higher trust = more frequent and better offers
TIP_OFFER_RATE: Frequency of incoming shadow market offers. Depends on trust level
TIP_TREASURY: Official city budget. Used for all public spending
TIP_OFFSHORE: Hidden offshore account. Funds from shadow deals, not visible to investigators
TIP_CW_INTEGRITY: Average information integrity across all districts. Higher = population trusts official sources
TIP_CW_COMPROMISED: Districts where enemy propaganda has significant influence
TIP_CW_COMMERCE: Commerce penalty from internet restrictions. Firewall or blackout reduce trade income
TIP_CW_HAPPINESS: Combined happiness penalty from blackouts, schedules, internet shutdowns, and neighbor envy
TIP_CW_EXODUS: Population leaving the city due to poor living conditions
TIP_CW_INFECTION: Average propaganda infection level. Higher = population more susceptible to enemy narratives
TIP_CW_RESISTANCE: Average resistance to propaganda. Higher = citizens better at rejecting false information
TIP_CW_TRAUMA: Average psychological trauma from war events. Increases over time during attacks
TIP_CW_PROTEST_RISK: Risk of civil unrest. Affected by happiness, propaganda, and blackout duration
TIP_CW_VULNERABLE: Households weakened by prolonged blackouts, making them susceptible to enemy propaganda
HELP_POWER:
Generation must match demand at all times. When supply falls short, grid frequency drops from 50 Hz and cascading failures begin.

Below 49.5 Hz -- stress zone: brownouts and voltage instability. Below 49.0 Hz -- critical zone where collapse stress builds within hours. At 48.5 Hz the grid collapses; the status line then shows POWER IN with a 24-hour countdown, and output returns to its target when it completes.

Winter tightens everything: attack waves come more often and repairs take longer. Damaged plants reduce capacity until repaired. Auto-dispatch sheds non-essential districts automatically, but VIP districts are always protected.

Your levers: build new capacity, repair damaged plants, manage district schedules, use backup reserves to bridge gaps.
HELP_BACKUP:
Three layers activate in sequence during blackouts: UPS batteries (instant), diesel generators (seconds delay), grid reconnect (minutes).

Discharge policy controls priority -- hospitals and schools are protected first. Battery capacity is shared; heavy blackouts drain reserves faster than recharge.

Generator coverage directly reduces blackout pressure on households. Covered families experience less psychological stress even during extended outages. However, generators create noise.

Plants at risk counts plants with 50%+ wear (not under repair) or carrying strike damage -- they may fail during the next attack.
HELP_MARKET:
The Emergency Power Market is an off-the-books channel: covert import buys megawatts when the grid is short, covert export sells surplus for cash. Both flows go through the same shadow contacts -- they share trust, share risk, and share consequences.

Covert Import covers deficits during attacks or breakdowns when legitimate generation cannot keep up. Pricing follows the shadow rate, not the wholesale market, so it is always more expensive than building real capacity. Use it as a bridge, not as a baseline.

Exposure Risk grows with import volume and how steadily you use the channel. Above moderate risk, audits trigger and the channel can freeze -- showing a status (Police Investigation, Low Trust, Temporary Punishment, Sanctions, Confiscated) instead of preset buttons. While frozen, the slot is unavailable until conditions clear.

Covert Export sells your surplus through the same network. Daily Yield depends on surplus size and current shadow rate -- it is volatile. Aggressive export percentages drain capacity that the city may need during the next strike, so plan around forecasted demand.

Running maximum import and maximum export at the same time is the fastest way to attract attention and lose both channels at once. Pulse usage during peak demand only and let exposure cool down between spikes.
HELP_DEFENSE:
Overlapping AA coverage zones increase intercept probability. Each type has different range: Heritage (short, cheap), Bofors (medium, reliable), Gepard (mobile, anti-drone), Patriot (long range, anti-missile).

Each station has its own magazine -- capacity depends on the type. Ammo depletes during attacks and resupplies automatically between waves. A station that runs dry mid-wave stops intercepting -- check magazines before the next wave.

Tall buildings block line of sight. Position stations with clear sightlines toward approach vectors, not just near targets. Coverage gaps are where strikes land.

Do not place AA directly next to the asset they protect. A successful intercept does not delete the threat in mid-air -- the wreckage falls. If a Shahed is shot down right above your power plant or substation, the burning debris lands on the AA itself. Position stations on the approach path, a short distance away from the protected target, so the debris field falls on open ground instead of on your own crew.

The enemy adapts -- if the same layout repeats, attack patterns shift to exploit weaknesses. Reposition between waves.
HELP_INTEL:
Attacks escalate through a tension system. Each wave increases tension toward the next tier -- harder compositions with more drones, missiles, and combined strikes.

Forecast accuracy depends on your intelligence level. Without intel you see arrival time only, not composition. Higher OPSEC means better early warning.

The enemy focuses on three categories: generation (power plants), infrastructure (substations), and residential (civilian areas). Focus shifts based on your defense patterns.

Source reports reveal exact composition before the wave. Evacuation orders reduce casualties but increase panic. High tension also raises covert import costs.
HELP_COGNITIVE:
Two attack channels drive infection: internet propaganda (constant when internet is on) and IPSO leaflet campaigns (targeted at specific districts).

Three defense sources: hero deployment (strongest, -50% infection), telemarathon broadcasts (city-wide but audience fatigues), and natural resistance from education.

Households under extended blackout (4+ hours) become much more vulnerable -- stressed people are easier to manipulate. This creates a feedback loop: blackouts -> vulnerability -> faster infection -> lower morale.

Key decisions: deploy hero to hotspots, choose telemarathon tone, disable internet in compromised districts (stops bots but cuts your own comms), and keep the lights on.
HELP_ENGINEERING:
Plants accumulate wear from operation and take separate operational damage from missile strikes. A plant can have low wear but heavy strike damage.

Wear above 50% is the danger zone: explosion risk grows with wear, and an explosion knocks out most of the plant's output. Exploded plants stay repairable -- just very expensive.

Repair is manual: the REPAIR button offers a Municipal contract (city budget, bureaucracy takes time) or Shadow Ops (your offshore cash, faster), with an optional kickback on the municipal route.

Thermal plants also need fuel -- a stockpile shortfall cuts output (FUEL column). Under low demand surplus capacity idles in spinning reserve (OUT column) and ramps back as demand returns.

Damage cascades: damaged plant -> reduced capacity -> remaining plants overloaded -> they wear faster. Prioritize repairing high-capacity plants first. Under-construction plants take days to come online -- plan expansion before the crisis, not during it.
HELP_SCHEMES:
Emergency Fund raids withdraw city reserves into your offshore account. Higher withdrawal rates drain faster but increase audit detection chance.

Fuel siphoning skims from plant deliveries -- steady profit but reduces efficiency and accelerates wear. Aggressive siphoning triggers equipment failures.

Detection probability grows with scheme intensity and total diverted funds. Getting caught costs ~20% shadow reputation -- contacts avoid unreliable partners.

Small consistent skimming is safer than large raids. Monitor investigation risk in the ledger before increasing intensity.
HELP_TRUST:
Trust directly controls procurement: how often offers appear and what equipment quality is available.

Below 25% -- frozen out: contacts stop calling until trust recovers. Above 50% -- regular offers, standard gear. Above 75% -- inner circle: frequent offers, premium equipment at better rates.

Getting caught in schemes costs ~20% trust. Declining an offer also costs a little (-5). Consistently completing deals builds trust over time.

Trust is the hidden multiplier: higher trust -> better procurement -> stronger defense -> fewer blackouts -> less infection.
HELP_LEDGER:
Treasury shows official city funds -- income from taxes, expenses on repairs, procurement, and salaries. Offshore account holds money diverted through schemes.

Large offshore balances attract investigators. When investigation risk crosses the threshold, auditors trigger prosecution.

Bankruptcy -- running out of treasury with debts outstanding -- triggers a crisis that can end the scenario.

Keep treasury reserves for post-attack repair spikes rather than draining everything offshore.
HELP_DISTRICTS:
Each district has its own demand, population, and management controls. You decide which stay powered, which enter blackouts, and which get VIP protection.

Scheduling modes: full power, rotating blackout windows, or full disconnection. Auto-dispatch automatically sheds lowest-priority districts when demand exceeds supply.

VIP districts are never shed by auto-dispatch -- use for critical areas with hospitals or key infrastructure. VIP bypass allows temporary override in extreme crises.

Disabling internet per-district cuts enemy bot propaganda but also your own telemarathon and communications -- a tradeoff between infection control and defense capability.
HELP_PROCUREMENT:
Maintenance contracts appear when plants need servicing. Choose between official vendor (expensive, 98% reliable) or shady vendor (cheap, ~70% quality + personal kickback).

Shady contracts save money and put a kickback offshore, but lower quality increases breakdown risk by up to 30%. Every shady deal adds corruption score.

Offer frequency depends on plant schedules and your shadow reputation. Higher trust means the contractor network is more active.

This is the entry to corruption: saved money and kickbacks fund shadow procurement for better equipment -- a calculated gamble with compounding risk.
HELP_DONOR:
International donors provide aid based on world attention (media coverage of your crisis) and trust index (how well you've managed previous aid).

Four attention tiers: Routine (minimal), Deep Concern (ammo resupply), Headlines (generator packages), Global Shock (maximum support, including Patriot systems). Shock rises from dramatic events -- major attacks, civilian casualties, infrastructure destruction.

Donor conferences let you formally request aid. Uses are limited -- spend during peak attention for maximum effect. Cooldown prevents repeated requests.

Mismanaging funds or corruption scandals reduce donor trust. High trust unlocks larger packages and faster delivery.
HELP_GLOBAL:
Five indicators show the city's health at a glance.

Frequency (Hz): grid stability. 50.0 = normal. Below 49.5 = stress zone. Below 49.0 = critical. At 48.5 the grid collapses. This is your most critical number.

Power Balance (MW): generation minus demand. Negative = deficit causing frequency drops. Battery shows remaining backup reserves.

Threshold: active means rolling blackouts are shedding load to prevent total collapse. Threat phase shows current enemy activity level.

When all indicators are green the city is stable. Red frequency + negative balance + active threshold = imminent crisis.

INTRO_TELEMETRY_DESC: Optional developer analytics: crash reports, FPS, memory, hardware snapshot, and a balance summary. Tied to a random ID, not your real identity. City and gameplay metrics that power AI news and stats are part of the functional Online stream, not this opt-out. No city names, save names, files, Steam ID, chat, email, or location.
UI_SETTINGS_ONLINE_DESC: Connects to the Global Grid: global AI news for all online players, online stats, leaderboards, and an optional nickname. Personal AI news are free during the beta and will need your own API key at release.
UI_SETTINGS_TELEMETRY_DESC: Optional developer analytics, opt-out, and only sent while Online is on: crash reports, FPS, memory, a one-time hardware snapshot, and a balance summary. These are tied to a random ID, not to your real identity. They do not control the functional Online stream: city and gameplay metrics still flow while Online is on, even with diagnostics off. No city names, save names, files, Steam ID, chat, email, or location.
UI_SETTINGS_TELEMETRY_NEEDS_ONLINE: Turn Online on first to change diagnostics.
ONLINE_CONSENT_INTRO: Online connects your city to the Global Grid. Your city data is sent to the server, tied to a random ID that is not your real identity, to generate and return online features to you:
ONLINE_CONSENT_FEATURE_NEWS: AI news and global digests for everyone (personal city digests are free in beta, your own API key at release)
ONLINE_CONSENT_FEATURE_NICKNAME: An optional leaderboard nickname (publicly visible if you set one)
ONLINE_CONSENT_DIAGNOSTICS: Developer diagnostics (crash reports, FPS, a hardware snapshot, balance metrics) are optional and separate from the functional data above. They are tied to a random ID, not to your real identity. Sent only while Online is on; turn off anytime in settings. The functional city/news stream above is unaffected by this choice.
